chapters.zh: 便雅悯地亚拿突城的祭司中，希勒家的儿子耶利米的话记在下面。
chapters.en: The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah, of the priests that in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin:

chapters.zh: 犹大王亚们的儿子约西亚在位十三年耶和华的话临到耶利米。
chapters.en: To whom the word of the LORD came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.

chapters.zh: 从犹大王约西亚的儿子约雅敬在位的时候，直到犹大王约西亚的儿子西底家在位的末年，就是十一年五月间耶路撒冷人被掳的时候，耶和华的话也常临到耶利米。
chapters.en: It came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah king of Judah, unto the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah the son of Josiah king of Judah, unto the carrying away of Jerusalem captive in the fifth month.

chapters.zh: 耶利米说，耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Then the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 我未将你造在腹中，我已晓得你；你未出母胎，我已分别你为圣；我已派你作列国的先知。
chapters.en: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee, I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations.

chapters.zh: 我就说：主耶和华啊，我不知怎样说，因为我是年幼的。
chapters.en: Then said I, Ah, Lord GOD! behold, I cannot speak: for I a child.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：你不要说我是年幼的，因为我差遣你到谁那里去，你都要去；我吩咐你说甚么话，你都要说。
chapters.en: But the LORD said unto me, Say not, I a child: for thou shalt go to all that I shall send thee, and whatsoever I command thee thou shalt speak.

chapters.zh: 你不要惧怕他们，因为我与你同在，要拯救你。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Be not afraid of their faces: for I with thee to deliver thee, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 于是耶和华伸手按我的口，对我说：我已将当说的话传给你。
chapters.en: Then the LORD put forth his hand, and touched my mouth. And the LORD said unto me, Behold, I have put my words in thy mouth.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我今日立你在列邦列国之上，为要施行拔出、拆毁、毁坏、倾覆，又要建立、栽植。
chapters.en: See, I have this day set thee over the nations and over the kingdoms, to root out, and to pull down, and to destroy, and to throw down, to build, and to plant.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话又临到我说：「耶利米，你看见甚么？」我说：「我看见一根杏树枝。」
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came unto me, saying, Jeremiah, what seest thou? And I said, I see a rod of an almond tree.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：「你看得不错；因为我留意保守我的话，使得成就。」
chapters.en: Then said the LORD unto me, Thou hast well seen: for I will hasten my word to perform it.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话第二次临到我说：「你看见甚么？」我说：「我看见一个烧开的锅，从北而倾。」
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me the second time, saying, What seest thou? And I said, I see a seething pot; and the face thereof toward the north.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：「必有灾祸从北方发出，临到这地的一切居民。」
chapters.en: Then the LORD said unto me, Out of the north an evil shall break forth upon all the inhabitants of the land.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：「看哪，我要召北方列国的众族；他们要来，各安座位在耶路撒冷的城门口，周围攻击城墙，又要攻击犹大的一切城邑。
chapters.en: For, lo, I will call all the families of the kingdoms of the north, saith the LORD; and they shall come, and they shall set every one his throne at the entering of the gates of Jerusalem, and against all the walls thereof round about, and against all the cities of Judah.

chapters.zh: 至于这民的一切恶，就是离弃我、向别神烧香、跪拜自己手所造的，我要发出我的判语，攻击他们。
chapters.en: And I will utter my judgments against them touching all their wickedness, who have forsaken me, and have burned incense unto other gods, and worshipped the works of their own hands.

chapters.zh: 所以你当束腰，起来将我所吩咐你的一切话告诉他们；不要因他们惊惶，免得我使你在他们面前惊惶。
chapters.en: Thou therefore gird up thy loins, and arise, and speak unto them all that I command thee: be not dismayed at their faces, lest I confound thee before them.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我今日使你成为坚城、铁柱、铜墙，与全地和犹大的君王、首领、祭司，并地上的众民反对。
chapters.en: For, behold, I have made thee this day a defenced city, and an iron pillar, and brasen walls against the whole land, against the kings of Judah, against the princes thereof, against the priests thereof, and against the people of the land.

chapters.zh: 他们要攻击你，却不能胜你；因为我与你同在，要拯救你。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And they shall fight against thee; but they shall not prevail against thee; for I with thee, saith the LORD, to deliver thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Moreover the word of the LORD came to me, saying,

chapters.zh: 你去向耶路撒冷人的耳中喊叫说，耶和华如此说：你幼年的恩爱，婚姻的爱情，你怎样在旷野，在未曾耕种之地跟随我，我都记得。
chapters.en: Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith the LORD; I remember thee, the kindness of thy youth, the love of thine espousals, when thou wentest after me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.

chapters.zh: 那时以色列归耶和华为圣，作为土产初熟的果子；凡吞吃他的必算为有罪，灾祸必临到他们。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Israel holiness unto the LORD, the firstfruits of his increase: all that devour him shall offend; evil shall come upon them, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 雅各家、以色列家的各族啊，你们当听耶和华的话。
chapters.en: Hear ye the word of the LORD, O house of Jacob, and all the families of the house of Israel:

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：你们的列祖见我有甚么不义，竟远离我，随从虚无的神，自己成为虚妄的呢？
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD, What iniquity have your fathers found in me, that they are gone far from me, and have walked after vanity, and are become vain?

chapters.zh: 他们也不说：那领我们从埃及地上来，引导我们经过旷野，沙漠有深坑之地，和干旱死荫、无人经过、无人居住之地的耶和华在哪里呢？
chapters.en: Neither said they, Where the LORD that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, that led us through the wilderness, through a land of deserts and of pits, through a land of drought, and of the shadow of death, through a land that no man passed through, and where no man dwelt?

chapters.zh: 我领你们进入肥美之地，使你们得吃其中的果子和美物；但你们进入的时候就玷污我的地，使我的产业成为可憎的。
chapters.en: And I brought you into a plentiful country, to eat the fruit thereof and the goodness thereof; but when ye entered, ye defiled my land, and made mine heritage an abomination.

chapters.zh: 祭司都不说，耶和华在哪里呢？传讲律法的都不认识我。官长违背我；先知藉巴力说预言，随从无益的神。
chapters.en: The priests said not, Where the LORD? and they that handle the law knew me not: the pastors also transgressed against me, and the prophets prophesied by Baal, and walked after do not profit.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我因此必与你们争辩，也必与你们的子孙争辩。
chapters.en: Wherefore I will yet plead with you, saith the LORD, and with your children's children will I plead.

chapters.zh: 你们且过到基提海岛去察看，打发人往基达去留心查考，看曾有这样的事没有。
chapters.en: For pass over the isles of Chittim, and see; and send unto Kedar, and consider diligently, and see if there be such a thing.

chapters.zh: 岂有一国换了他的神吗？其实这不是神！但我的百姓将他们的荣耀换了那无益的神。
chapters.en: Hath a nation changed gods, which yet no gods? but my people have changed their glory for doth not profit.

chapters.zh: 诸天哪，要因此惊奇，极其恐慌，甚为凄凉！这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Be astonished, O ye heavens, at this, and be horribly afraid, be ye very desolate, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为我的百姓做了两件恶事，就是离弃我这活水的泉源，为自己凿出池子，是破裂不能存水的池子。
chapters.en: For my people have committed two evils; they have forsaken me the fountain of living waters, hewed them out cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.

chapters.zh: 以色列是仆人吗？是家中生的奴仆吗？为何成为掠物呢？
chapters.en: Israel a servant? he a homeborn ? why is he spoiled?

chapters.zh: 少壮狮子向他咆哮，大声吼叫，使他的地荒凉；城邑也都焚烧，无人居住。
chapters.en: The young lions roared upon him, yelled, and they made his land waste: his cities are burned without inhabitant.

chapters.zh: 挪弗人和答比匿人也打破你的头顶。
chapters.en: Also the children of Noph and Tahapanes have broken the crown of thy head.

chapters.zh: 这事临到你身上，不是你自招的吗？不是因耶和华 ─ 你神引你行路的时候，你离弃他吗？
chapters.en: Hast thou not procured this unto thyself, in that thou hast forsaken the LORD thy God, when he led thee by the way?

chapters.zh: 现今你为何在埃及路上要喝西曷的水呢？你为何在亚述路上要喝大河的水呢？
chapters.en: And now what hast thou to do in the way of Egypt, to drink the waters of Sihor? or what hast thou to do in the way of Assyria, to drink the waters of the river?

chapters.zh: 你自己的恶必惩治你；你背道的事必责备你。由此可知可见，你离弃耶和华 ─ 你的神，不存敬畏我的心，乃为恶事，为苦事。这是主 ─ 万军之耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thine own wickedness shall correct thee, and thy backslidings shall reprove thee: know therefore and see that an evil and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the LORD thy God, and that my fear not in thee, saith the Lord GOD of hosts.

chapters.zh: 我在古时折断你的轭，解开你的绳索。你说：我必不事奉耶和华；因为你在各高冈上、各青翠树下屈身行淫（或译：我在古时折断你的轭，解开你的绳索，你就说：我必不事奉别神。谁知你在各高冈上、各青翠树下仍屈身行淫）。
chapters.en: For of old time I have broken thy yoke, burst thy bands; and thou saidst, I will not transgress; when upon every high hill and under every green tree thou wanderest, playing the harlot.

chapters.zh: 然而，我栽你是上等的葡萄树，全然是真种子；你怎么向我变为外邦葡萄树的坏枝子呢？
chapters.en: Yet I had planted thee a noble vine, wholly a right seed: how then art thou turned into the degenerate plant of a strange vine unto me?

chapters.zh: 你虽用碱、多用肥皂洗濯，你罪孽的痕迹仍然在我面前显出。这是主耶和华说的。
chapters.en: For though thou wash thee with nitre, and take thee much soap, thine iniquity is marked before me, saith the Lord GOD.

chapters.zh: 你怎能说：我没有玷污、没有随从众巴力？你看你谷中的路，就知道你所行的如何。你是快行的独峰驼，狂奔乱走。
chapters.en: How canst thou say, I am not polluted, I have not gone after Baalim? see thy way in the valley, know what thou hast done: a swift dromedary traversing her ways;

chapters.zh: 你是野驴，惯在旷野，欲心发动就吸风；起性的时候谁能使牠转去呢？凡寻找牠的必不致疲乏；在牠的月分必能寻见。
chapters.en: A wild ass used to the wilderness, snuffeth up the wind at her pleasure; in her occasion who can turn her away? all they that seek her will not weary themselves; in her month they shall find her.

chapters.zh: 我说：你不要使脚上无鞋，喉咙干渴。你倒说：这是枉然。我喜爱别神，我必随从他们。
chapters.en: Withhold thy foot from being unshod, and thy throat from thirst: but thou saidst, There is no hope: no; for I have loved strangers, and after them will I go.

chapters.zh: 贼被捉拿，怎样羞愧，﹞ 以色列家和他们的君王、首领、祭司、先知也都照样羞愧。
chapters.en: As the thief is ashamed when he is found, so is the house of Israel ashamed; they, their kings, their princes, and their priests, and their prophets,

chapters.zh: 他们向木头说：你是我的父；向石头说：你是生我的。他们以背向我，不以面向我；及至遭遇患难的时候却说：起来拯救我们。
chapters.en: Saying to a stock, Thou my father; and to a stone, Thou hast brought me forth: for they have turned back unto me, and not face: but in the time of their trouble they will say, Arise, and save us.

chapters.zh: 你为自己做的神在哪里呢？你遭遇患难的时候，叫他们起来拯救你罢！犹大啊，你神的数目与你城的数目相等。
chapters.en: But where thy gods that thou hast made thee? let them arise, if they can save thee in the time of thy trouble: for the number of thy cities are thy gods, O Judah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：你们为何与我争辩呢？你们都违背了我。
chapters.en: Wherefore will ye plead with me? ye all have transgressed against me, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我责打你们的儿女是徒然的，他们不受惩治。你们自己的刀吞灭你们的先知，好像残害的狮子。
chapters.en: In vain have I smitten your children; they received no correction: your own sword hath devoured your prophets, like a destroying lion.

chapters.zh: 这世代的人哪，你们要看明耶和华的话。我岂向以色列作旷野呢？或作幽暗之地呢？我的百姓为何说：我们脱离约束，再不归向你了？
chapters.en: O generation, see ye the word of the LORD. Have I been a wilderness unto Israel? a land of darkness? wherefore say my people, We are lords; we will come no more unto thee?

chapters.zh: 处女岂能忘记他的妆饰呢？新妇岂能忘记他的美衣呢？我的百姓却忘记了我无数的日子！
chapters.en: Can a maid forget her ornaments, a bride her attire? yet my people have forgotten me days without number.

chapters.zh: 你怎么修饰你的道路要求爱情呢？就是恶劣的妇人你也叫他们行你的路。
chapters.en: Why trimmest thou thy way to seek love? therefore hast thou also taught the wicked ones thy ways.

chapters.zh: 并且你的衣襟上有无辜穷人的血；你杀他们并不是遇见他们挖窟窿，乃是因这一切的事。
chapters.en: Also in thy skirts is found the blood of the souls of the poor innocents: I have not found it by secret search, but upon all these.

chapters.zh: 你还说：我无辜；耶和华的怒气必定向我消了。看哪，我必审问你；因你自说：我没有犯罪。
chapters.en: Yet thou sayest, Because I am innocent, surely his anger shall turn from me. Behold, I will plead with thee, because thou sayest, I have not sinned.

chapters.zh: 你为何东跑西奔要更换你的路呢？你必因埃及蒙羞，像从前因亚述蒙羞一样。
chapters.en: Why gaddest thou about so much to change thy way? thou also shalt be ashamed of Egypt, as thou wast ashamed of Assyria.

chapters.zh: 你也必两手抱头从埃及出来；因为耶和华已经弃绝你所倚靠的，你必不因他们得顺利。
chapters.en: Yea, thou shalt go forth from him, and thine hands upon thine head: for the LORD hath rejected thy confidences, and thou shalt not prosper in them.

chapters.zh: 有话说：人若休妻，妻离他而去，作了别人的妻，前夫岂能再收回他来？若收回他来，那地岂不是大大玷污了么？但你和许多亲爱的行邪淫，还可以归向我。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: They say, If a man put away his wife, and she go from him, and become another man's, shall he return unto her again? shall not that land be greatly polluted? but thou hast played the harlot with many lovers; yet return again to me, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你向净光的高处举目观看，你在何处没有淫行呢？你坐在道旁等候，好像亚拉伯人在旷野埋伏一样，并且你的淫行邪恶玷污了全地。
chapters.en: Lift up thine eyes unto the high places, and see where thou hast not been lien with. In the ways hast thou sat for them, as the Arabian in the wilderness; and thou hast polluted the land with thy whoredoms and with thy wickedness.

chapters.zh: 因此甘霖停止，春（原文是晚）雨不降。你还是有娼妓之脸，不顾羞耻。
chapters.en: Therefore the showers have been withholden, and there hath been no latter rain; and thou hadst a whore's forehead, thou refusedst to be ashamed.

chapters.zh: 从今以后，你岂不向我呼叫说：我父啊，你是我幼年的恩主。
chapters.en: Wilt thou not from this time cry unto me, My father, thou the guide of my youth?

chapters.zh: 耶和华岂永远怀怒，存留到底吗？看哪，你又发恶言又行坏事，随自己的私意而行（或译：你虽这样说，还是行恶放纵欲心）。
chapters.en: Will he reserve for ever? will he keep to the end? Behold, thou hast spoken and done evil things as thou couldest.

chapters.zh: 约西亚王在位的时候，耶和华又对我说：「背道的以色列所行的，你看见没有？他上各高山，在各青翠树下行淫。
chapters.en: The LORD said also unto me in the days of Josiah the king, Hast thou seen which backsliding Israel hath done? she is gone up upon every high mountain and under every green tree, and there hath played the harlot.

chapters.zh: 他行这些事以后，我说他必归向我，他却不归向我。他奸诈的妹妹犹大也看见了。
chapters.en: And I said after she had done all these , Turn thou unto me. But she returned not. And her treacherous sister Judah saw .

chapters.zh: 背道的以色列行淫，我为这缘故给他休书休他；我看见他奸诈的妹妹犹大，还不惧怕，也去行淫。
chapters.en: And I saw, when for all the causes whereby backsliding Israel committed adultery I had put her away, and given her a bill of divorce; yet her treacherous sister Judah feared not, but went and played the harlot also.

chapters.zh: 因以色列轻忽了他的淫乱，和石头木头行淫，地就被玷污了。
chapters.en: And it came to pass through the lightness of her whoredom, that she defiled the land, and committed adultery with stones and with stocks.

chapters.zh: 虽有这一切的事，他奸诈的妹妹犹大还不一心归向我，不过是假意归我。这是耶和华说的。」
chapters.en: And yet for all this her treacherous sister Judah hath not turned unto me with her whole heart, but feignedly, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：「背道的以色列比奸诈的犹大还显为义。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto me, The backsliding Israel hath justified herself more than treacherous Judah.

chapters.zh: 你去向北方宣告说：耶和华说：背道的以色列啊，回来吧！我必不怒目看你们；因为我是慈爱的，我必不永远存怒。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Go and proclaim these words toward the north, and say, Return, thou backsliding Israel, saith the LORD; I will not cause mine anger to fall upon you: for I merciful, saith the LORD, I will not keep for ever.

chapters.zh: 只要承认你的罪孽，就是你违背耶和华 ─ 你的神，在各青翠树下向别神东奔西跑，没有听从我的话。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Only acknowledge thine iniquity, that thou hast transgressed against the LORD thy God, and hast scattered thy ways to the strangers under every green tree, and ye have not obeyed my voice, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：背道的儿女啊，回来吧！因为我作你们的丈夫，并且我必将你们从一城取一人，从一族取两人，带到锡安。
chapters.en: Turn, O backsliding children, saith the LORD; for I am married unto you: and I will take you one of a city, and two of a family, and I will bring you to Zion:

chapters.zh: 我也必将合我心的牧者赐给你们。他们必以知识和智慧牧养你们。」
chapters.en: And I will give you pastors according to mine heart, which shall feed you with knowledge and understanding.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：「你们在国中生养众多；当那些日子，人必不再提说耶和华的约柜，不追想，不记念，不觉缺少，也不再制造。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, when ye be multiplied and increased in the land, in those days, saith the LORD, they shall say no more, The ark of the covenant of the LORD: neither shall it come to mind: neither shall they remember it; neither shall they visit ; neither shall be done any more.

chapters.zh: 那时，人必称耶路撒冷为耶和华的宝座；万国必到耶路撒冷，在耶和华立名的地方聚集。他们必不再随从自己顽梗的恶心行事。
chapters.en: At that time they shall call Jerusalem the throne of the LORD; and all the nations shall be gathered unto it, to the name of the LORD, to Jerusalem: neither shall they walk any more after the imagination of their evil heart.

chapters.zh: 当那些日子，犹大家要和以色列家同行，从北方之地一同来到我赐给你们列祖为业之地。」
chapters.en: In those days the house of Judah shall walk with the house of Israel, and they shall come together out of the land of the north to the land that I have given for an inheritance unto your fathers.

chapters.zh: 我说：我怎样将你安置在儿女之中，赐给你美地，就是万国中肥美的产业。我又说：你们必称我为父，也不再转去不跟从我。
chapters.en: But I said, How shall I put thee among the children, and give thee a pleasant land, a goodly heritage of the hosts of nations? and I said, Thou shalt call me, My father; and shalt not turn away from me.

chapters.zh: 以色列家，你们向我行诡诈，真像妻子行诡诈离开他丈夫一样。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Surely a wife treacherously departeth from her husband, so have ye dealt treacherously with me, O house of Israel, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 在净光的高处听见人声，就是以色列人哭泣恳求之声，乃因他们走弯曲之道，忘记耶和华 ─ 他们的神。
chapters.en: A voice was heard upon the high places, weeping supplications of the children of Israel: for they have perverted their way, they have forgotten the LORD their God.

chapters.zh: 你们这背道的儿女啊，回来吧！我要医治你们背道的病。看哪，我们来到你这里，因你是耶和华 ─ 我们的神。
chapters.en: Return, ye backsliding children, I will heal your backslidings. Behold, we come unto thee; for thou the LORD our God.

chapters.zh: 仰望从小山或从大山的喧嚷中得帮助，真是枉然的。以色列得救，诚然在乎耶和华 ─ 我们的神。
chapters.en: Truly in vain from the hills, the multitude of mountains: truly in the LORD our God the salvation of Israel.

chapters.zh: 从我们幼年以来，那可耻的偶像将我们列祖所劳碌得来的羊群、牛群，和他们的儿女都吞吃了。
chapters.en: For shame hath devoured the labour of our fathers from our youth; their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.

chapters.zh: 我们在羞耻中躺卧罢！愿惭愧将我们遮盖；因为从立国（原文是幼年）以来，我们和我们的列祖常常得罪耶和华 ─ 我们的神，没有听从耶和华 ─ 我们神的话。
chapters.en: We lie down in our shame, and our confusion covereth us: for we have sinned against the LORD our God, we and our fathers, from our youth even unto this day, and have not obeyed the voice of the LORD our God.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：以色列啊，你若回来归向我，若从我眼前除掉你可憎的偶像，你就不被迁移。
chapters.en: If thou wilt return, O Israel, saith the LORD, return unto me: and if thou wilt put away thine abominations out of my sight, then shalt thou not remove.

chapters.zh: 你必凭诚实、公平、公义，指著永生的耶和华起誓；列国必因耶和华称自己为有福，也必因他夸耀。
chapters.en: And thou shalt swear, The LORD liveth, in truth, in judgment, and in righteousness; and the nations shall bless themselves in him, and in him shall they glory.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对犹大和耶路撒冷人如此说：要开垦你们的荒地，不要撒种在荆棘中。
chapters.en: For thus saith the LORD to the men of Judah and Jerusalem, Break up your fallow ground, and sow not among thorns.

chapters.zh: 犹大人和耶路撒冷的居民哪，你们当自行割礼，归耶和华，将心里的污秽除掉；恐怕我的忿怒因你们的恶行发作，如火著起，甚至无人能以熄灭！
chapters.en: Circumcise yourselves to the LORD, and take away the foreskins of your heart, ye men of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem: lest my fury come forth like fire, and burn that none can quench , because of the evil of your doings.

chapters.zh: 你们当传扬在犹大，宣告在耶路撒冷说：你们当在国中吹角，高声呼叫说：你们当聚集！我们好进入坚固城！
chapters.en: Declare ye in Judah, and publish in Jerusalem; and say, Blow ye the trumpet in the land: cry, gather together, and say, Assemble yourselves, and let us go into the defenced cities.

chapters.zh: 应当向锡安竖立大旗。要逃避，不要迟延，因我必使灾祸与大毁灭从北方来到。
chapters.en: Set up the standard toward Zion: retire, stay not: for I will bring evil from the north, and a great destruction.

chapters.zh: 有狮子从密林中上来，是毁坏列国的。他已经动身出离本处，要使你的地荒凉，使你的城邑变为荒场无人居住。
chapters.en: The lion is come up from his thicket, and the destroyer of the Gentiles is on his way; he is gone forth from his place to make thy land desolate; thy cities shall be laid waste, without an inhabitant.

chapters.zh: 因此，你们当腰束麻布，大声哀号，因为耶和华的烈怒没有向我们转消。
chapters.en: For this gird you with sackcloth, lament and howl: for the fierce anger of the LORD is not turned back from us.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：到那时，君王和首领的心都要消灭；祭司都要惊奇，先知都要诧异。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass at that day, saith the LORD, the heart of the king shall perish, and the heart of the princes; and the priests shall be astonished, and the prophets shall wonder.

chapters.zh: 我说：哀哉！主耶和华啊，你真是大大地欺哄这百姓和耶路撒冷，说：你们必得平安。其实刀剑害及性命了。
chapters.en: Then said I, Ah, Lord GOD! surely thou hast greatly deceived this people and Jerusalem, saying, Ye shall have peace; whereas the sword reacheth unto the soul.

chapters.zh: 那时，必有话对这百姓和耶路撒冷说：有一阵热风从旷野净光的高处向我的众民（原文是民女）刮来，不是为簸扬，也不是为扬净。
chapters.en: At that time shall it be said to this people and to Jerusalem, A dry wind of the high places in the wilderness toward the daughter of my people, not to fan, nor to cleanse,

chapters.zh: 必有一阵更大的风从这些地方为我刮来；现在我又必发出判语，攻击他们。
chapters.en: a full wind from those shall come unto me: now also will I give sentence against them.

chapters.zh: 看哪，仇敌必如云上来；他的战车如旋风，他的马匹比鹰更快。我们有祸了！我们败落了！
chapters.en: Behold, he shall come up as clouds, and his chariots as a whirlwind: his horses are swifter than eagles. Woe unto us! for we are spoiled.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷啊，你当洗去心中的恶，使你可以得救。恶念存在你心里要到几时呢？
chapters.en: O Jerusalem, wash thine heart from wickedness, that thou mayest be saved. How long shall thy vain thoughts lodge within thee?

chapters.zh: 有声音从但传扬，从以法莲山报祸患。
chapters.en: For a voice declareth from Dan, and publisheth affliction from mount Ephraim.

chapters.zh: 你们当传给列国，报告攻击耶路撒冷的事说：有探望的人从远方来到，向犹大的城邑大声呐喊。
chapters.en: Make ye mention to the nations; behold, publish against Jerusalem, watchers come from a far country, and give out their voice against the cities of Judah.

chapters.zh: 他们周围攻击耶路撒冷，好像看守田园的，因为他背叛了我。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: As keepers of a field, are they against her round about; because she hath been rebellious against me, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你的行动，你的作为，招惹这事；这是你罪恶的结果，实在是苦，是害及你心了！
chapters.en: Thy way and thy doings have procured these unto thee; this thy wickedness, because it is bitter, because it reacheth unto thine heart.

chapters.zh: 我的肺腑啊，我的肺腑啊，我心疼痛！我心在我著面烦躁不安。我不能静默不言，因为我已经听见角声和打仗的喊声。
chapters.en: My bowels, my bowels! I am pained at my very heart; my heart maketh a noise in me; I cannot hold my peace, because thou hast heard, O my soul, the sound of the trumpet, the alarm of war.

chapters.zh: 毁坏的信息连络不绝，因为全地荒废。我的帐棚忽然毁坏；我的幔子顷刻破裂。
chapters.en: Destruction upon destruction is cried; for the whole land is spoiled: suddenly are my tents spoiled, my curtains in a moment.

chapters.zh: 我看见大旗，听见角声，要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long shall I see the standard, hear the sound of the trumpet?

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我的百性愚顽，不认识我；他们是愚昧无知的儿女，有智慧行恶，没有知识行善。
chapters.en: For my people foolish, they have not known me; they sottish children, and they have none understanding: they wise to do evil, but to do good they have no knowledge.

chapters.zh: 先知说：我观看地，不料，地是空虚混沌；我观看天，天也无光。
chapters.en: I beheld the earth, and, lo, without form, and void; and the heavens, and they no light.

chapters.zh: 我观看大山，不料，尽都震动，小山也都摇来摇去。
chapters.en: I beheld the mountains, and, lo, they trembled, and all the hills moved lightly.

chapters.zh: 我观看，不料，无人；空中的飞鸟也都躲避。
chapters.en: I beheld, and, lo, no man, and all the birds of the heavens were fled.

chapters.zh: 我观看，不料，肥田变为荒地；一切城邑在耶和华面前，因他的烈怒都被拆毁。
chapters.en: I beheld, and, lo, the fruitful place a wilderness, and all the cities thereof were broken down at the presence of the LORD, by his fierce anger.

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：全地必然荒凉，我却不毁灭净尽。
chapters.en: For thus hath the LORD said, The whole land shall be desolate; yet will I not make a full end.

chapters.zh: 因此，地要悲哀，在上的天也必黑暗；因为我言已出，我意已定，必不后悔，也不转意不做。
chapters.en: For this shall the earth mourn, and the heavens above be black: because I have spoken , I have purposed , and will not repent, neither will I turn back from it.

chapters.zh: 各城的人因马兵和弓箭手的响声就都逃跑，进入密林，爬上磐石；各城被撇下，无人住在其中。
chapters.en: The whole city shall flee for the noise of the horsemen and bowmen; they shall go into thickets, and climb up upon the rocks: every city forsaken, and not a man dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 你凄凉的时候要怎样行呢？你虽穿上朱红衣服，佩戴黄金装饰，用颜料修饰眼目，这样标致是枉然的！恋爱你的藐视你，并且寻索你的性命。
chapters.en: And thou spoiled, what wilt thou do? Though thou clothest thyself with crimson, though thou deckest thee with ornaments of gold, though thou rentest thy face with painting, in vain shalt thou make thyself fair; lovers will despise thee, they will seek thy life.

chapters.zh: 我听见有声音，彷佛妇人产难的声音，好像生头胎疼痛的声音，是锡安女子（就是指民的意思）的声音；他喘著气、挓抄手，说：我有祸了！在杀人的跟前，我的心发昏了。
chapters.en: For I have heard a voice as of a woman in travail, the anguish as of her that bringeth forth her first child, the voice of the daughter of Zion, bewaileth herself, spreadeth her hands, , Woe me now! for my soul is wearied because of murderers.

chapters.zh: 你们当在耶路撒冷的街上跑来跑去，在宽阔处寻找，看看有一人行公义求诚实没有？若有，我就赦免这城。
chapters.en: Run ye to and fro through the streets of Jerusalem, and see now, and know, and seek in the broad places thereof, if ye can find a man, if there be that executeth judgment, that seeketh the truth; and I will pardon it.

chapters.zh: 其中的人虽然指著永生的耶和华起誓，所起的誓实在是假的。
chapters.en: And though they say, The LORD liveth; surely they swear falsely.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你的眼目不是看顾诚实么？你击打他们，他们却不伤恸；你毁灭他们，他们仍不受惩治。他们使脸刚硬过于磐石，不肯回头。
chapters.en: O LORD, not thine eyes upon the truth? thou hast stricken them, but they have not grieved; thou hast consumed them, they have refused to receive correction: they have made their faces harder than a rock; they have refused to return.

chapters.zh: 我说：这些人实在是贫穷的，是愚昧的，因为不晓得耶和华的作为和他们神的法则。
chapters.en: Therefore I said, Surely these poor; they are foolish: for they know not the way of the LORD, the judgment of their God.

chapters.zh: 我要去见尊大的人，对他们说话，因为他们晓得耶和华的作为和他们神的法则。哪知，这些人齐心将轭折断，挣开绳索。
chapters.en: I will get me unto the great men, and will speak unto them; for they have known the way of the LORD, the judgment of their God: but these have altogether broken the yoke, burst the bonds.

chapters.zh: 因此，林中的狮子必害死他们；晚上（或译：野地）的豺狼必灭绝他们；豹子要在城外窥伺他们。凡出城的必被撕碎；因为他们的罪过极多，背道的事也加增了。
chapters.en: Wherefore a lion out of the forest shall slay them, a wolf of the evenings shall spoil them, a leopard shall watch over their cities: every one that goeth out thence shall be torn in pieces: because their transgressions are many, their backslidings are increased.

chapters.zh: 我怎能赦免你呢？你的儿女离弃我，又指著那不是神的起誓。我使他们饱足，他们就行奸淫，成群地聚集在娼妓家里。
chapters.en: How shall I pardon thee for this? thy children have forsaken me, and sworn by no gods: when I had fed them to the full, they then committed adultery, and assembled themselves by troops in the harlots' houses.

chapters.zh: 他们像喂饱的马到处乱跑，各向他邻舍的妻发嘶声。
chapters.en: They were fed horses in the morning: every one neighed after his neighbour's wife.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我岂不因这些事讨罪呢？岂不报复这样的国民呢？
chapters.en: Shall I not visit for these ? saith the LORD: and shall not my soul be avenged on such a nation as this?

chapters.zh: 你们要上他葡萄园的墙施行毁坏，但不可毁坏净尽，只可除掉他的枝子，因为不属耶和华。
chapters.en: Go ye up upon her walls, and destroy; but make not a full end: take away her battlements; for they not the LORD'S.

chapters.zh: 原来以色列家和犹大家大行诡诈攻击我。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: For the house of Israel and the house of Judah have dealt very treacherously against me, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他们不认耶和华，说：这并不是他，灾祸必不临到我们；刀剑和饥荒，我们也看不见。
chapters.en: They have belied the LORD, and said, not he; neither shall evil come upon us; neither shall we see sword nor famine:

chapters.zh: 先知的话必成为风；道也不在他们里面。这灾必临到他们身上。
chapters.en: And the prophets shall become wind, and the word not in them: thus shall it be done unto them.

chapters.zh: 所以耶和华 ─ 万军之神如此说：因为百姓说这话，我必使我的话在你口中为火，使他们为柴；这火便将他们烧灭。
chapters.en: Wherefore thus saith the LORD God of hosts, Because ye speak this word, behold, I will make my words in thy mouth fire, and this people wood, and it shall devour them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：以色列家啊，我必使一国的民从远方来攻击你，是强盛的国，是从古而有的国。他们的言语你不晓得，他们的话你不明白。
chapters.en: Lo, I will bring a nation upon you from far, O house of Israel, saith the LORD: it a mighty nation, it an ancient nation, a nation whose language thou knowest not, neither understandest what they say.

chapters.zh: 他们的箭袋是敞开的坟墓；他们都是勇士。
chapters.en: Their quiver as an open sepulchre, they all mighty men.

chapters.zh: 他们必吃尽你的庄稼和你的粮食，是你儿女该吃的；必吃尽你的牛羊，吃尽你的葡萄和无花果；又必用刀毁坏你所倚靠的坚固城。
chapters.en: And they shall eat up thine harvest, and thy bread, thy sons and thy daughters should eat: they shall eat up thy flocks and thine herds: they shall eat up thy vines and thy fig trees: they shall impoverish thy fenced cities, wherein thou trustedst, with the sword.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：就是到那时，我也不将你们毁灭净尽。
chapters.en: Nevertheless in those days, saith the LORD, I will not make a full end with you.

chapters.zh: 百姓若说：耶和华 ─ 我们的神为甚么向我们行这一切事呢？你就对他们说：你们怎样离弃耶和华（原文是我），在你们的地上事奉外邦神，也必照样在不属你们的地上事奉外邦人。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, when ye shall say, Wherefore doeth the LORD our God all these unto us? then shalt thou answer them, Like as ye have forsaken me, and served strange gods in your land, so shall ye serve strangers in a land not yours.

chapters.zh: 当传扬在雅各家，报告在犹大说：
chapters.en: Declare this in the house of Jacob, and publish it in Judah, saying,

chapters.zh: 愚昧无知的百姓啊，你们有眼不看，有耳不听，现在当听这话。
chapters.en: Hear now this, O foolish people, and without understanding; which have eyes, and see not; which have ears, and hear not:

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：你们怎么不惧怕我呢？我以永远的定例，用沙为海的界限，水不得越过。因此，你们在我面前还不战兢么？波浪虽然翻腾，却不能逾越；虽然匉訇，却不能过去。
chapters.en: Fear ye not me? saith the LORD: will ye not tremble at my presence, which have placed the sand the bound of the sea by a perpetual decree, that it cannot pass it: and though the waves thereof toss themselves, yet can they not prevail; though they roar, yet can they not pass over it?

chapters.zh: 但这百姓有背叛忤逆的心；他们叛我而去，
chapters.en: But this people hath a revolting and a rebellious heart; they are revolted and gone.

chapters.zh: 心内也不说：我们应当敬畏耶和华 ─ 我们的神；他按时赐雨，就是秋雨春雨，又为我们定收割的节令，永存不废。
chapters.en: Neither say they in their heart, Let us now fear the LORD our God, that giveth rain, both the former and the latter, in his season: he reserveth unto us the appointed weeks of the harvest.

chapters.zh: 你们的罪孽使这些事转离你们；你们的罪恶使你们不能得福。
chapters.en: Your iniquities have turned away these , and your sins have withholden good from you.

chapters.zh: 因为在我民中有恶人。他们埋伏窥探，好像捕鸟的人；他们设立圈套陷害人。
chapters.en: For among my people are found wicked : they lay wait, as he that setteth snares; they set a trap, they catch men.

chapters.zh: 笼内怎样满了雀鸟，他们的房中也照样充满诡诈；所以他们得成为大，而且富足。
chapters.en: As a cage is full of birds, so their houses full of deceit: therefore they are become great, and waxen rich.

chapters.zh: 他们肥胖光润，作恶过甚，不为人伸冤！就是不为孤儿伸冤，不使他亨通，也不为穷人辨屈。
chapters.en: They are waxen fat, they shine: yea, they overpass the deeds of the wicked: they judge not the cause, the cause of the fatherless, yet they prosper; and the right of the needy do they not judge.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我岂不因这些事讨罪呢？岂不报复这样的国民呢？
chapters.en: Shall I not visit for these ? saith the LORD: shall not my soul be avenged on such a nation as this?

chapters.zh: 国中有可惊骇、可憎恶的事：
chapters.en: A wonderful and horrible thing is committed in the land;

chapters.zh: 就是先知说假预言，祭司藉他们把持权柄；我的百姓也喜爱这些事，到了结局你们怎样行呢？
chapters.en: The prophets prophesy falsely, and the priests bear rule by their means; and my people love so: and what will ye do in the end thereof?

chapters.zh: 便雅悯人哪，你们要逃出耶路撒冷，在提哥亚吹角，在伯哈基琳立号旗；因为有灾祸与大毁灭从北方张望。
chapters.en: O ye children of Benjamin, gather yourselves to flee out of the midst of Jerusalem, and blow the trumpet in Tekoa, and set up a sign of fire in Bethhaccerem: for evil appeareth out of the north, and great destruction.

chapters.zh: 那秀美娇嫩的锡安女子（就是指民的意思），我必剪除。
chapters.en: I have likened the daughter of Zion to a comely and delicate .

chapters.zh: 牧人必引他们的羊群到他那著，在他周围支搭帐棚，各在自己所占之地使羊吃草。
chapters.en: The shepherds with their flocks shall come unto her; they shall pitch tents against her round about; they shall feed every one in his place.

chapters.zh: 你们要准备攻击他。起来罢，我们可以趁午时上去。哀哉！日已渐斜，晚影拖长了。
chapters.en: Prepare ye war against her; arise, and let us go up at noon. Woe unto us! for the day goeth away, for the shadows of the evening are stretched out.

chapters.zh: 起来罢，我们夜间上去，毁坏他的宫殿。
chapters.en: Arise, and let us go by night, and let us destroy her palaces.

chapters.zh: 因为万军之耶和华如此说：你们要砍伐树木，筑垒攻打耶路撒冷。这就是那该罚的城，其中尽是欺压。
chapters.en: For thus hath the LORD of hosts said, Hew ye down trees, and cast a mount against Jerusalem: this the city to be visited; she wholly oppression in the midst of her.

chapters.zh: 井怎样涌出水来，这城也照样涌出恶来；在其间常听见有强暴毁灭的事，病患损伤也常在我面前。
chapters.en: As a fountain casteth out her waters, so she casteth out her wickedness: violence and spoil is heard in her; before me continually grief and wounds.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷啊，你当受教，免得我心与你生疏，免得我使你荒凉，成为无人居住之地。
chapters.en: Be thou instructed, O Jerusalem, lest my soul depart from thee; lest I make thee desolate, a land not inhabited.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华曾如此说：敌人必掳尽以色列剩下的民，如同摘净葡萄一样。你要像摘葡萄的人摘了又摘，回手放在筐子里。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD of hosts, They shall throughly glean the remnant of Israel as a vine: turn back thine hand as a grapegatherer into the baskets.

chapters.zh: 现在我可以向谁说话作见证，使他们听呢？他们的耳朵未受割礼，不能听见。看哪，耶和华的话他们以为羞辱，不以为喜悦。
chapters.en: To whom shall I speak, and give warning, that they may hear? behold, their ear uncircumcised, and they cannot hearken: behold, the word of the LORD is unto them a reproach; they have no delight in it.

chapters.zh: 因此我被耶和华的忿怒充满，难以含忍。我要倾在街中的孩童和聚会的少年人身上，连夫带妻，并年老的与日子满足的都必被擒拿。
chapters.en: Therefore I am full of the fury of the LORD; I am weary with holding in: I will pour it out upon the children abroad, and upon the assembly of young men together: for even the husband with the wife shall be taken, the aged with full of days.

chapters.zh: 他们的房屋、田地，和妻子都必转归别人；我要伸手攻击这地的居民。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And their houses shall be turned unto others, fields and wives together: for I will stretch out my hand upon the inhabitants of the land, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 因为他们从最小的到至大的都一味地贪婪，从先知到祭司都行事虚谎。
chapters.en: For from the least of them even unto the greatest of them every one given to covetousness; and from the prophet even unto the priest every one dealeth falsely.

chapters.zh: 他们轻轻忽忽地医治我百姓的损伤，说：平安了！平安了！其实没有平安。
chapters.en: They have healed also the hurt of my people slightly, saying, Peace, peace; when no peace.

chapters.zh: 他们行可憎的事知道惭愧么？不然，他们毫不惭愧，也不知羞耻。因此，他们必在仆倒的人中仆倒；我向他们讨罪的时候，他们必致跌倒。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? nay, they were not at all ashamed, neither could they blush: therefore they shall fall among them that fall: at the time I visit them they shall be cast down, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：你们当站在路上察看，访问古道，哪是善道，便行在其间；这样，你们心里必得安息。他们却说：我们不行在其间。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD, Stand ye in the ways, and see, and ask for the old paths, where the good way, and walk therein, and ye shall find rest for your souls. But they said, We will not walk .

chapters.zh: 我设立守望的人照管你们，说：要听角声。他们却说：我们不听。
chapters.en: Also I set watchmen over you, , Hearken to the sound of the trumpet. But they said, We will not hearken.

chapters.zh: 列国啊，因此你们当听！会众啊，要知道他们必遭遇的事。
chapters.en: Therefore hear, ye nations, and know, O congregation, what among them.

chapters.zh: 地啊，当听！我必使灾祸临到这百姓，就是他们意念所结的果子；因为他们不听从我的言语，至于我的训诲（或译：律法），他们也厌弃了。
chapters.en: Hear, O earth: behold, I will bring evil upon this people, the fruit of their thoughts, because they have not hearkened unto my words, nor to my law, but rejected it.

chapters.zh: 从示巴出的乳香，从远方出的菖蒲（或译：甘蔗）奉来给我有何益呢？你们的燔祭不蒙悦纳；你们的平安祭，我也不喜悦。
chapters.en: To what purpose cometh there to me incense from Sheba, and the sweet cane from a far country? your burnt offerings not acceptable, nor your sacrifices sweet unto me.

chapters.zh: 所以耶和华如此说：我要将绊脚石放在这百姓前面。父亲和儿子要一同跌在其上；邻舍与朋友也都灭亡。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD, Behold, I will lay stumblingblocks before this people, and the fathers and the sons together shall fall upon them; the neighbour and his friend shall perish.

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：看哪，有一种民从北方而来，并有一大国被激动，从地极来到。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD, Behold, a people cometh from the north country, and a great nation shall be raised from the sides of the earth.

chapters.zh: 他们拿弓和枪，性情残忍，不施怜悯；他们的声音像海浪匉訇。锡安城（原文是女子）啊，他们骑马都摆队伍，如上战场的人要攻击你。
chapters.en: They shall lay hold on bow and spear; they cruel, and have no mercy; their voice roareth like the sea; and they ride upon horses, set in array as men for war against thee, O daughter of Zion.

chapters.zh: 我们听见他们的风声，手就发软；痛苦将我们抓住，疼痛彷佛产难的妇人。
chapters.en: We have heard the fame thereof: our hands wax feeble: anguish hath taken hold of us, pain, as of a woman in travail.

chapters.zh: 你们不要往田野去，也不要行在路上，因四围有仇敌的刀剑和惊吓。
chapters.en: Go not forth into the field, nor walk by the way; for the sword of the enemy fear on every side.

chapters.zh: 我民（原文是民女）哪，应当腰束麻布，滚在灰中。你要悲伤，如丧独生子痛痛哭号，因为灭命的要忽然临到我们。
chapters.en: O daughter of my people, gird with sackcloth, and wallow thyself in ashes: make thee mourning, an only son, most bitter lamentation: for the spoiler shall suddenly come upon us.

chapters.zh: 我使你在我民中为高台（或译：试验人的），为保障，使你知道试验他们的行动。
chapters.en: I have set thee a tower a fortress among my people, that thou mayest know and try their way.

chapters.zh: 他们都是极悖逆的，往来谗谤人。他们是铜是铁，都行坏事。
chapters.en: They all grievous revolters, walking with slanders: brass and iron; they all corrupters.

chapters.zh: 风箱吹火，铅被烧毁；他们炼而又炼，终是徒然；因为恶劣的还未除掉。
chapters.en: The bellows are burned, the lead is consumed of the fire; the founder melteth in vain: for the wicked are not plucked away.

chapters.zh: 人必称他们为被弃的银渣，因为耶和华已经弃掉他们。
chapters.en: Reprobate silver shall call them, because the LORD hath rejected them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到耶利米说：
chapters.en: The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying,

chapters.zh: 你当站在耶和华殿的门口，在那著宣传这话说：你们进这些门敬拜耶和华的一切犹大人，当听耶和华的话。
chapters.en: Stand in the gate of the LORD'S house, and proclaim there this word, and say, Hear the word of the LORD, all Judah, that enter in at these gates to worship the LORD.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华 ─ 以色列的神如此说：你们改正行动作为，我就使你们在这地方仍然居住。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, Amend your ways and your doings, and I will cause you to dwell in this place.

chapters.zh: 你们不要倚靠虚谎的话，说：这些是耶和华的殿，是耶和华的殿，是耶和华的殿！
chapters.en: Trust ye not in lying words, saying, The temple of the LORD, The temple of the LORD, The temple of the LORD, these.

chapters.zh: 你们若实在改正行动作为，在人和邻舍中间诚然施行公平，
chapters.en: For if ye throughly amend your ways and your doings; if ye throughly execute judgment between a man and his neighbour;

chapters.zh: 不欺压寄居的和孤儿寡妇；在这地方不流无辜人的血，也不随从别神陷害自己；
chapters.en: ye oppress not the stranger, the fatherless, and the widow, and shed not innocent blood in this place, neither walk after other gods to your hurt:

chapters.zh: 我就使你们在这地方仍然居住，就是我古时所赐给你们列祖的地，直到永远。
chapters.en: Then will I cause you to dwell in this place, in the land that I gave to your fathers, for ever and ever.

chapters.zh: 看哪，你们倚靠虚谎无益的话。
chapters.en: Behold, ye trust in lying words, that cannot profit.

chapters.zh: 你们偷盗，杀害，奸淫，起假誓，向巴力烧香，并随从素不认识的别神，
chapters.en: Will ye steal, murder, and commit adultery, and swear falsely, and burn incense unto Baal, and walk after other gods whom ye know not;

chapters.zh: 且来到这称为我名下的殿，在我面前敬拜；又说：我们可以自由了。你们这样的举动是要行那些可憎的事么？
chapters.en: And come and stand before me in this house, which is called by my name, and say, We are delivered to do all these abominations?

chapters.zh: 这称为我名下的殿在你们眼中岂可看为贼窝么？我都看见了。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Is this house, which is called by my name, become a den of robbers in your eyes? Behold, even I have seen , saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们且往示罗去，就是我先前立为我名的居所，察看我因这百姓以色列的罪恶向那地所行的如何。
chapters.en: But go ye now unto my place which in Shiloh, where I set my name at the first, and see what I did to it for the wickedness of my people Israel.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：现在因你们行了这一切的事，我也从早起来警戒你们，你们却不听从；呼唤你们，你们却不答应。
chapters.en: And now, because ye have done all these works, saith the LORD, and I spake unto you, rising up early and speaking, but ye heard not; and I called you, but ye answered not;

chapters.zh: 所以我要向这称我为名下、你们所倚靠的殿，与我所赐给你们和你们列祖的地施行，照我从前向示罗所行的一样。
chapters.en: Therefore will I do unto house, which is called by my name, wherein ye trust, and unto the place which I gave to you and to your fathers, as I have done to Shiloh.

chapters.zh: 我必将你们从我眼前赶出，正如赶出你们的众弟兄，就是以法莲的一切后裔。
chapters.en: And I will cast you out of my sight, as I have cast out all your brethren, the whole seed of Ephraim.

chapters.zh: 所以，你不要为这百姓祈祷；不要为他们呼求祷告，也不要向我为他们祈求，因我不听允你。
chapters.en: Therefore pray not thou for this people, neither lift up cry nor prayer for them, neither make intercession to me: for I will not hear thee.

chapters.zh: 他们在犹大城邑中和耶路撒冷街上所行的，你没有看见么？
chapters.en: Seest thou not what they do in the cities of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem?

chapters.zh: 孩子捡柴，父亲烧火，妇女抟面做饼，献给天后，又向别神浇奠祭，惹我发怒。
chapters.en: The children gather wood, and the fathers kindle the fire, and the women knead dough, to make cakes to the queen of heaven, and to pour out drink offerings unto other gods, that they may provoke me to anger.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：他们岂是惹我发怒呢？不是自己惹祸，以致脸上惭愧么？
chapters.en: Do they provoke me to anger? saith the LORD: not themselves to the confusion of their own faces?

chapters.zh: 所以主耶和华如此说：看哪，我必将我的怒气和忿怒倾在这地方的人和牲畜身上，并田野的树木和地著的出产上，必如火著起，不能熄灭。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the Lord GOD; Behold, mine anger and my fury shall be poured out upon this place, upon man, and upon beast, and upon the trees of the field, and upon the fruit of the ground; and it shall burn, and shall not be quenched.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华 ─ 以色列的神如此说：你们将燔祭加在平安祭上，吃肉罢！
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel; Put your burnt offerings unto your sacrifices, and eat flesh.

chapters.zh: 因为我将你们列祖从埃及地领出来的那日，燔祭平安祭的事我并没有提说，也没有吩咐他们。
chapters.en: For I spake not unto your fathers, nor commanded them in the day that I brought them out of the land of Egypt, concerning burnt offerings or sacrifices:

chapters.zh: 我只吩咐他们这一件说：你们当听从我的话，我就作你们的神，你们也作我的子民。你们行我所吩咐的一切道，就可以得福。
chapters.en: But this thing commanded I them, saying, Obey my voice, and I will be your God, and ye shall be my people: and walk ye in all the ways that I have commanded you, that it may be well unto you.

chapters.zh: 他们却不听从，不侧耳而听，竟随从自己的计谋和顽梗的恶心，向后不向前。
chapters.en: But they hearkened not, nor inclined their ear, but walked in the counsels in the imagination of their evil heart, and went backward, and not forward.

chapters.zh: 自从你们列祖出埃及地的那日，直到今日，我差遣我的仆人众先知到你们那里去，每日从早起来差遣他们。
chapters.en: Since the day that your fathers came forth out of the land of Egypt unto this day I have even sent unto you all my servants the prophets, daily rising up early and sending :

chapters.zh: 你们却不听从，不侧耳而听，竟硬著颈项行恶，比你们列祖更甚。
chapters.en: Yet they hearkened not unto me, nor inclined their ear, but hardened their neck: they did worse than their fathers.

chapters.zh: 你要将这一切的话告诉他们，他们却不听从；呼唤他们，他们却不答应。
chapters.en: Therefore thou shalt speak all these words unto them; but they will not hearken to thee: thou shalt also call unto them; but they will not answer thee.

chapters.zh: 你要对他们说：这就是不听从耶和华 ─ 他们神的话、不受教训的国民；从他们的口中，诚实灭绝了。
chapters.en: But thou shalt say unto them, This a nation that obeyeth not the voice of the LORD their God, nor receiveth correction: truth is perished, and is cut off from their mouth.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷啊，要剪发抛弃，在净光的高处举哀；因为耶和华丢掉离弃了惹他忿怒的世代。
chapters.en: Cut off thine hair, , and cast away, and take up a lamentation on high places; for the LORD hath rejected and forsaken the generation of his wrath.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：犹大人行我眼中看为恶的事，将可憎之物设立在称为我名下的殿中，污秽这殿。
chapters.en: For the children of Judah have done evil in my sight, saith the LORD: they have set their abominations in the house which is called by my name, to pollute it.

chapters.zh: 他们在欣嫩子谷建筑陀斐特的邱坛，好在火中焚烧自己的儿女。这并不是我所吩咐的，也不是我心所起的意。
chapters.en: And they have built the high places of Tophet, which in the valley of the son of Hinnom, to burn their sons and their daughters in the fire; which I commanded not, neither came it into my heart.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：因此，日子将到，这地方不再称为陀斐特和欣嫩子谷，反倒称为杀戮谷。因为要在陀斐特葬埋尸首，甚至无处可葬；
chapters.en: Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that it shall no more be called Tophet, nor the valley of the son of Hinnom, but the valley of slaughter: for they shall bury in Tophet, till there be no place.

chapters.zh: 并且这百姓的尸首必给空中的飞鸟和地上的野兽作食物，并无人哄赶。
chapters.en: And the carcases of this people shall be meat for the fowls of the heaven, and for the beasts of the earth; and none shall fray away.

chapters.zh: 那时，我必使犹大城邑中和耶路撒冷街上，欢喜和快乐的声音，新郎和新妇的声音，都止息了，因为地必成为荒场。
chapters.en: Then will I cause to cease from the cities of Judah, and from the streets of Jerusalem, the voice of mirth, and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom, and the voice of the bride: for the land shall be desolate.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：到那时，人必将犹大王的骸骨和他首领的骸骨、祭司的骸骨、先知的骸骨，并耶路撒冷居民的骸骨，都从坟墓中取出来，
chapters.en: At that time, saith the LORD, they shall bring out the bones of the kings of Judah, and the bones of his princes, and the bones of the priests, and the bones of the prophets, and the bones of the inhabitants of Jerusalem, out of their graves:

chapters.zh: 抛散在日头、月亮，和天上众星之下，就是他们从前所喜爱、所事奉、所随从、所求问、所敬拜的。这些骸骨不再收殓，不再葬埋，必在地面上成为粪土；
chapters.en: And they shall spread them before the sun, and the moon, and all the host of heaven, whom they have loved, and whom they have served, and after whom they have walked, and whom they have sought, and whom they have worshipped: they shall not be gathered, nor be buried; they shall be for dung upon the face of the earth.

chapters.zh: 并且这恶族所剩下的民在我所赶他们到的各处，宁可拣死不拣生。这是万军之耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And death shall be chosen rather than life by all the residue of them that remain of this evil family, which remain in all the places whither I have driven them, saith the LORD of hosts.

chapters.zh: 你要对他们说，耶和华如此说：人跌倒，不再起来么？人转去，不再转来么？
chapters.en: Moreover thou shalt say unto them, Thus saith the LORD; Shall they fall, and not arise? shall he turn away, and not return?

chapters.zh: 这耶路撒冷的民，为何恒久背道呢？他们守定诡诈，不肯回头。
chapters.en: Why is this people of Jerusalem slidden back by a perpetual backsliding? they hold fast deceit, they refuse to return.

chapters.zh: 我留心听，听见他们说不正直的话。无人悔改恶行，说：我做的是甚么呢？他们各人转奔己路，如马直闯战场。
chapters.en: I hearkened and heard, they spake not aright: no man repented him of his wickedness, saying, What have I done? every one turned to his course, as the horse rusheth into the battle.

chapters.zh: 空中的鹳鸟知道来去的定期；斑鸠燕子与白鹤也守候当来的时令；我的百姓却不知道耶和华的法则。
chapters.en: Yea, the stork in the heaven knoweth her appointed times; and the turtle and the crane and the swallow observe the time of their coming; but my people know not the judgment of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们怎么说：我们有智慧，耶和华的律法在我们这里？看哪，文士的假笔舞弄虚假。
chapters.en: How do ye say, We wise, and the law of the LORD with us? Lo, certainly in vain made he ; the pen of the scribes in vain.

chapters.zh: 智慧人惭愧，惊惶，被擒拿；他们弃掉耶和华的话，心里还有甚么智慧呢？
chapters.en: The wise are ashamed, they are dismayed and taken: lo, they have rejected the word of the LORD; and what wisdom in them?

chapters.zh: 所以我必将他们的妻子给别人，将他们的田地给别人为业；因为他们从最小的到至大的都一味地贪婪，从先知到祭司都行事虚谎。
chapters.en: Therefore will I give their wives unto others, their fields to them that shall inherit : for every one from the least even unto the greatest is given to covetousness, from the prophet even unto the priest every one dealeth falsely.

chapters.zh: 他们轻轻忽忽地医治我百姓的损伤，说：平安了！平安了！其实没有平安。
chapters.en: For they have healed the hurt of the daughter of my people slightly, saying, Peace, peace; when no peace.

chapters.zh: 他们行可憎的事知道惭愧么？不然，他们毫不惭愧，也不知羞耻。因此他们必在仆倒的人中仆倒；我向他们讨罪的时候，他们必致跌倒。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Were they ashamed when they had committed abomination? nay, they were not at all ashamed, neither could they blush: therefore shall they fall among them that fall: in the time of their visitation they shall be cast down, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我必使他们全然灭绝；葡萄树上必没有葡萄，无花果树上必没有果子，叶子也必枯干。我所赐给他们的，必离开他们过去。
chapters.en: I will surely consume them, saith the LORD: no grapes on the vine, nor figs on the fig tree, and the leaf shall fade; and I have given them shall pass away from them.

chapters.zh: 我们为何静坐不动呢？我们当聚集，进入坚固城，在那里静默不言；因为耶和华 ─ 我们的神使我们静默不言，又将苦胆水给我们喝，都因我们得罪了耶和华。
chapters.en: Why do we sit still? assemble yourselves, and let us enter into the defenced cities, and let us be silent there: for the LORD our God hath put us to silence, and given us water of gall to drink, because we have sinned against the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我们指望平安，却得不著好处；指望痊愈的时候，不料，受了惊惶。
chapters.en: We looked for peace, but no good for a time of health, and behold trouble!

chapters.zh: 听见从但那里敌人的马喷鼻气，他的壮马发嘶声，全地就都震动；因为他们来吞灭这地和其上所有的，吞灭这城与其中的居民。
chapters.en: The snorting of his horses was heard from Dan: the whole land trembled at the sound of the neighing of his strong ones; for they are come, and have devoured the land, and all that is in it; the city, and those that dwell therein.

chapters.zh: 看哪，我必使毒蛇到你们中间，是不服法术的，必咬你们。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: For, behold, I will send serpents, cockatrices, among you, which not charmed, and they shall bite you, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 我有忧愁，愿能自慰；我心在我里面发昏。
chapters.en: I would comfort myself against sorrow, my heart faint in me.

chapters.zh: 听啊，是我百姓的哀声从极远之地而来，说：耶和华不在锡安么？锡安的王不在其中么？耶和华说：他们为甚么以雕刻的偶像和外邦虚无的神惹我发怒呢？
chapters.en: Behold the voice of the cry of the daughter of my people because of them that dwell in a far country: not the LORD in Zion? not her king in her? Why have they provoked me to anger with their graven images, with strange vanities?

chapters.zh: 麦秋已过，夏令已完，我们还未得救！
chapters.en: The harvest is past, the summer is ended, and we are not saved.

chapters.zh: 先知说：因我百姓的损伤，我也受了损伤。我哀痛，惊惶将我抓住。
chapters.en: For the hurt of the daughter of my people am I hurt; I am black; astonishment hath taken hold on me.

chapters.zh: 在基列岂没有乳香呢？在那著岂没有医生呢？我百姓为何不得痊愈呢？
chapters.en: no balm in Gilead; no physician there? why then is not the health of the daughter of my people recovered?

chapters.zh: 但愿我的头为水，我的眼为泪的泉源，我好为我百姓（原文是民女；七节同）中被杀的人昼夜哭泣。
chapters.en: Oh that my head were waters, and mine eyes a fountain of tears, that I might weep day and night for the slain of the daughter of my people!

chapters.zh: 惟愿我在旷野有行路人住宿之处，使我可以离开我的民出去；因他们都是行奸淫的，是行诡诈的一党。
chapters.en: Oh that I had in the wilderness a lodging place of wayfaring men; that I might leave my people, and go from them! for they all adulterers, an assembly of treacherous men.

chapters.zh: 他们弯起舌头像弓一样，为要说谎话。他们在国中增长势力，不是为行诚实，乃是恶上加恶，并不认识我。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: And they bend their tongues their bow lies: but they are not valiant for the truth upon the earth; for they proceed from evil to evil, and they know not me, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们各人当谨防邻舍，不可信靠弟兄；因为弟兄尽行欺骗，邻舍都往来谗谤人。
chapters.en: Take ye heed every one of his neighbour, and trust ye not in any brother: for every brother will utterly supplant, and every neighbour will walk with slanders.

chapters.zh: 他们各人欺哄邻舍，不说真话；他们教舌头学习说谎，劳劳碌碌地作孽。
chapters.en: And they will deceive every one his neighbour, and will not speak the truth: they have taught their tongue to speak lies, weary themselves to commit iniquity.

chapters.zh: 你的住处在诡诈的人中；他们因行诡诈，不肯认识我。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: Thine habitation in the midst of deceit; through deceit they refuse to know me, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 所以万军之耶和华如此说：看哪，我要将他们融化熬炼；不然，我因我百姓的罪该怎样行呢？
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD of hosts, Behold, I will melt them, and try them; for how shall I do for the daughter of my people?

chapters.zh: 他们的舌头是毒箭，说话诡诈；人与邻舍口说和平话，心却谋害他。
chapters.en: Their tongue an arrow shot out; it speaketh deceit: speaketh peaceably to his neighbour with his mouth, but in heart he layeth his wait.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我岂不因这些事讨他们的罪呢？岂不报复这样的国民呢？
chapters.en: Shall I not visit them for these ? saith the LORD: shall not my soul be avenged on such a nation as this?

chapters.zh: 我要为山岭哭泣悲哀，为旷野的草场扬声哀号；因为都已干焦，甚至无人经过。人也听不见牲畜鸣叫，空中的飞鸟和地上的野兽都已逃去。
chapters.en: For the mountains will I take up a weeping and wailing, and for the habitations of the wilderness a lamentation, because they are burned up, so that none can pass through ; neither can hear the voice of the cattle; both the fowl of the heavens and the beast are fled; they are gone.

chapters.zh: 我必使耶路撒冷变为乱堆，为野狗的住处，也必使犹大的城邑变为荒场，无人居住。
chapters.en: And I will make Jerusalem heaps, a den of dragons; and I will make the cities of Judah desolate, without an inhabitant.

chapters.zh: 谁是智慧人，可以明白这事？耶和华的口向谁说过，使他可以传说？遍地为何灭亡，干焦好像旷野，甚至无人经过呢？
chapters.en: Who the wise man, that may understand this? and to whom the mouth of the LORD hath spoken, that he may declare it, for what the land perisheth is burned up like a wilderness, that none passeth through?

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：因为这百姓离弃我，在他们面前所设立的律法没有遵行，也没有听从我的话；
chapters.en: And the LORD saith, Because they have forsaken my law which I set before them, and have not obeyed my voice, neither walked therein;

chapters.zh: 只随从自己顽梗的心行事，照他们列祖所教训的随从众巴力。
chapters.en: But have walked after the imagination of their own heart, and after Baalim, which their fathers taught them:

chapters.zh: 所以万军之耶和华 ─ 以色列的神如此说：看哪，我必将茵 𫈟 给这百姓吃，又将苦胆水给他们喝。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel; Behold, I will feed them, this people, with wormwood, and give them water of gall to drink.

chapters.zh: 我要把他们散在列邦中，就是他们和他们列祖素不认识的列邦。我也要使刀剑追杀他们，直到将他们灭尽。
chapters.en: I will scatter them also among the heathen, whom neither they nor their fathers have known: and I will send a sword after them, till I have consumed them.

chapters.zh: 万军之耶和华如此说：你们应当思想，将善唱哀歌的妇女召来，又打发人召善哭的妇女来，
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD of hosts, Consider ye, and call for the mourning women, that they may come; and send for cunning , that they may come:

chapters.zh: 叫他们速速为我们举哀，使我们眼泪汪汪，使我们的眼皮涌出水来。
chapters.en: And let them make haste, and take up a wailing for us, that our eyes may run down with tears, and our eyelids gush out with waters.

chapters.zh: 因为听见哀声出于锡安，说：我们怎样败落了！我们大大地惭愧！我们撇下地土；人也拆毁了我们的房屋。
chapters.en: For a voice of wailing is heard out of Zion, How are we spoiled! we are greatly confounded, because we have forsaken the land, because our dwellings have cast out.

chapters.zh: 妇女们哪，你们当听耶和华的话，领受他口中的言语；又当教导你们的儿女举哀，各人教导邻舍唱哀歌。
chapters.en: Yet hear the word of the LORD, O ye women, and let your ear receive the word of his mouth, and teach your daughters wailing, and every one her neighbour lamentation.

chapters.zh: 因为死亡上来，进了我们的窗户，入了我们的宫殿；要从外边剪除孩童，从街上剪除少年人。
chapters.en: For death is come up into our windows, is entered into our palaces, to cut off the children from without, the young men from the streets.

chapters.zh: 你当说，耶和华如此说：人的尸首必倒在田野像粪土，又像收割的人遗落的一把禾稼，无人收取。
chapters.en: Speak, Thus saith the LORD, Even the carcases of men shall fall as dung upon the open field, and as the handful after the harvestman, and none shall gather .

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：智慧人不要因他的智慧夸口，勇士不要因他的勇力夸口，财主不要因他的财物夸口。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD, Let not the wise glory in his wisdom, neither let the mighty glory in his might, let not the rich glory in his riches:

chapters.zh: 夸口的却因他有聪明，认识我是耶和华，又知道我喜悦在世上施行慈爱、公平，和公义，以此夸口。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: But let him that glorieth glory in this, that he understandeth and knoweth me, that I the LORD which exercise lovingkindness, judgment, and righteousness, in the earth: for in these I delight, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：看哪，日子将到，我要刑罚一切受过割礼、心却未受割礼的，
chapters.en: Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will punish all circumcised with the uncircumcised;

chapters.zh: 就是埃及、犹大、以东、亚扪人、摩押人，和一切住在旷野剃周围头发的；因为列国人都没有受割礼，以色列人心中也没有受割礼。
chapters.en: Egypt, and Judah, and Edom, and the children of Ammon, and Moab, and all in the utmost corners, that dwell in the wilderness: for all nations uncircumcised, and all the house of Israel uncircumcised in the heart.

chapters.zh: 以色列家啊，要听耶和华对你们所说的话。
chapters.en: Hear ye the word which the LORD speaketh unto you, O house of Israel:

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：你们不要效法列国的行为，也不要为天象惊惶，因列国为此事惊惶。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD, Learn not the way of the heathen, and be not dismayed at the signs of heaven; for the heathen are dismayed at them.

chapters.zh: 众民的风俗是虚空的；他们在树林中用斧子砍伐一棵树，匠人用手工造成偶像。
chapters.en: For the customs of the people vain: for cutteth a tree out of the forest, the work of the hands of the workman, with the axe.

chapters.zh: 他们用金银妆饰他，用钉子和锤子钉稳，使他不动摇。
chapters.en: They deck it with silver and with gold; they fasten it with nails and with hammers, that it move not.

chapters.zh: 他好像棕树，是旋成的，不能说话，不能行走，必须有人抬著。你们不要怕他；他不能降祸，也无力降福。
chapters.en: They upright as the palm tree, but speak not: they must needs be borne, because they cannot go. Be not afraid of them; for they cannot do evil, neither also in them to do good.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，没有能比你的！你本为大，有大能大力的名。
chapters.en: Forasmuch as none like unto thee, O LORD; thou great, and thy name great in might.

chapters.zh: 万国的王啊，谁不敬畏你？敬畏你本是合宜的；因为在列国的智慧人中，虽有政权的尊荣，也不能比你。
chapters.en: Who would not fear thee, O King of nations? for to thee doth it appertain: forasmuch as among all the wise of the nations, and in all their kingdoms, none like unto thee.

chapters.zh: 他们尽都是畜类，是愚昧的。偶像的训诲算甚么呢？偶像不过是木头。
chapters.en: But they are altogether brutish and foolish: the stock a doctrine of vanities.

chapters.zh: 有银子打成片，是从他施带来的，并有从乌法来的金子，都是匠人和银匠的手工，又有蓝色紫色料的衣服，都是巧匠的工作。
chapters.en: Silver spread into plates is brought from Tarshish, and gold from Uphaz, the work of the workman, and of the hands of the founder: blue and purple their clothing: they all the work of cunning .

chapters.zh: 惟耶和华是真神，是活神，是永远的王。他一发怒，大地震动；他一恼恨，列国都担当不起。
chapters.en: But the LORD the true God, he the living God, and an everlasting king: at his wrath the earth shall tremble, and the nations shall not be able to abide his indignation.

chapters.zh: 你们要对他们如此说：不是那创造天地的神，必从地上从天下被除灭！）
chapters.en: Thus shall ye say unto them, The gods that have not made the heavens and the earth, they shall perish from the earth, and from under these heavens.

chapters.zh: 耶和华用能力创造大地，用智慧建立世界，用聪明铺张穹苍。
chapters.en: He hath made the earth by his power, he hath established the world by his wisdom, and hath stretched out the heavens by his discretion.

chapters.zh: 他一发声，空中便有多水激动；他使云雾从地极上腾；他造电随雨而闪，从他府库中带出风来。
chapters.en: When he uttereth his voice, a multitude of waters in the heavens, and he causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth; he maketh lightnings with rain, and bringeth forth the wind out of his treasures.

chapters.zh: 各人都成了畜类，毫无知识；各银匠都因他雕刻的偶像羞愧。他所铸的偶像本是虚假的，其中并无气息，
chapters.en: Every man is brutish in knowledge: every founder is confounded by the graven image: for his molten image falsehood, and no breath in them.

chapters.zh: 都是虚无的，是迷惑人的工作；到追讨的时候必被除灭。
chapters.en: They vanity, the work of errors: in the time of their visitation they shall perish.

chapters.zh: 雅各的分不像这些，因他是造作万有的主。以色列也是他产业的支派，万军之耶和华是他的名。
chapters.en: The portion of Jacob not like them: for he the former of all ; and Israel the rod of his inheritance: The LORD of hosts his name.

chapters.zh: 受围困的人哪，当收拾你的财物，从国中带出去。
chapters.en: Gather up thy wares out of the land, O inhabitant of the fortress.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华如此说：这时候，我必将此地的居民，好像用机弦甩出去，又必加害在他们身上，使他们觉悟。
chapters.en: For thus saith the LORD, Behold, I will sling out the inhabitants of the land at this once, and will distress them, that they may find .

chapters.zh: 民说：祸哉！我受损伤；我的伤痕极其重大。我却说：这真是我的痛苦，必须忍受。
chapters.en: Woe is me for my hurt! my wound is grievous: but I said, Truly this a grief, and I must bear it.

chapters.zh: 我的帐棚毁坏；我的绳索折断。我的儿女离我出去，没有了。无人再支搭我的帐棚，挂起我的幔子。
chapters.en: My tabernacle is spoiled, and all my cords are broken: my children are gone forth of me, and they not: none to stretch forth my tent any more, and to set up my curtains.

chapters.zh: 因为牧人都成为畜类，没有求问耶和华，所以不得顺利；他们的羊群也都分散。
chapters.en: For the pastors are become brutish, and have not sought the LORD: therefore they shall not prosper, and all their flocks shall be scattered.

chapters.zh: 有风声！看哪，敌人来了！有大扰乱从北方出来，要使犹大城邑变为荒凉，成为野狗的住处。
chapters.en: Behold, the noise of the bruit is come, and a great commotion out of the north country, to make the cities of Judah desolate, a den of dragons.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我晓得人的道路不由自己，行路的人也不能定自己的脚步。
chapters.en: O LORD, I know that the way of man not in himself: not in man that walketh to direct his steps.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，求你从宽惩治我，不要在你的怒中惩治我，恐怕使我归于无有。
chapters.en: O LORD, correct me, but with judgment; not in thine anger, lest thou bring me to nothing.

chapters.zh: 愿你将忿怒倾在不认识你的列国中，和不求告你名的各族上；因为他们吞了雅各，不但吞了，而且灭绝，把他的住处变为荒场。
chapters.en: Pour out thy fury upon the heathen that know thee not, and upon the families that call not on thy name: for they have eaten up Jacob, and devoured him, and consumed him, and have made his habitation desolate.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到耶利米说：
chapters.en: The word that came to Jeremiah from the LORD, saying,

chapters.zh: 当听这约的话，告诉犹大人和耶路撒冷的居民，
chapters.en: Hear ye the words of this covenant, and speak unto the men of Judah, and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem;

chapters.zh: 对他们说，耶和华 ─ 以色列的神如此说：不听从这约之话的人必受咒诅。
chapters.en: And say thou unto them, Thus saith the LORD God of Israel; Cursed the man that obeyeth not the words of this covenant,

chapters.zh: 这约是我将你们列祖从埃及地领出来、脱离铁炉的那日所吩咐他们的，说：你们要听从我的话，照我一切所吩咐的去行。这样，你们就作我的子民，我也作你们的神；
chapters.en: Which I commanded your fathers in the day I brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, from the iron furnace, saying, Obey my voice, and do them, according to all which I command you: so shall ye be my people, and I will be your God:

chapters.zh: 我好坚定向你们列祖所起的誓，给他们流奶与蜜之地，正如今日一样。我就回答说：耶和华啊，阿们！
chapters.en: That I may perform the oath which I have sworn unto your fathers, to give them a land flowing with milk and honey, as this day. Then answered I, and said, So be it, O LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：你要在犹大城邑中和耶路撒冷街市上，宣告这一切话说：你们当听从遵行这约的话。
chapters.en: Then the LORD said unto me, Proclaim all these words in the cities of Judah, and in the streets of Jerusalem, saying, Hear ye the words of this covenant, and do them.

chapters.zh: 因为我将你们列祖从埃及地领出来的那日，直到今日，都是从早起来，切切诰诫他们说：你们当听从我的话。
chapters.en: For I earnestly protested unto your fathers in the day I brought them up out of the land of Egypt, unto this day, rising early and protesting, saying, Obey my voice.

chapters.zh: 他们却不听从，不侧耳而听，竟随从自己顽梗的恶心去行。所以我使这约中一切咒诅的话临到他们身上；这约是我吩咐他们行的，他们却不去行。
chapters.en: Yet they obeyed not, nor inclined their ear, but walked every one in the imagination of their evil heart: therefore I will bring upon them all the words of this covenant, which I commanded to do; but they did not.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：在犹大人和耶路撒冷居民中有同谋背叛的事。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto me, A conspiracy is found among the men of Judah, and among the inhabitants of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 他们转去效法他们的先祖，不肯听我的话，犯罪作孽，又随从别神，事奉他。以色列家和犹大家背了我与他们列祖所立的约。
chapters.en: They are turned back to the iniquities of their forefathers, which refused to hear my words; and they went after other gods to serve them: the house of Israel and the house of Judah have broken my covenant which I made with their fathers.

chapters.zh: 所以耶和华如此说：我必使灾祸临到他们，是他们不能逃脱的。他们必向我哀求，我却不听。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD, Behold, I will bring evil upon them, which they shall not be able to escape; and though they shall cry unto me, I will not hearken unto them.

chapters.zh: 那时，犹大城邑的人和耶路撒冷的居民要去哀求他们烧香所供奉的神；只是遭难的时候，这些神毫不拯救他们。
chapters.en: Then shall the cities of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem go, and cry unto the gods unto whom they offer incense: but they shall not save them at all in the time of their trouble.

chapters.zh: 犹大啊，你神的数目与你城的数目相等；你为那可耻的巴力所筑烧香的坛也与耶路撒冷街道的数目相等。
chapters.en: For the number of thy cities were thy gods, O Judah; and the number of the streets of Jerusalem have ye set up altars to shameful thing, altars to burn incense unto Baal.

chapters.zh: 所以你不要为这百姓祈祷，不要为他们呼求祷告；因为他们遭难向我哀求的时候，我必不应允。
chapters.en: Therefore pray not thou for this people, neither lift up a cry or prayer for them: for I will not hear in the time that they cry unto me for their trouble.

chapters.zh: 我所亲爱的，既行许多淫乱，圣肉也离了你，你在我殿中做甚么呢？你作恶就喜乐。
chapters.en: What hath my beloved to do in mine house, she hath wrought lewdness with many, and the holy flesh is passed from thee? when thou doest evil, then thou rejoicest.

chapters.zh: 从前耶和华给你起名叫青橄榄树，又华美又结好果子；如今他用哄嚷之声，点火在其上，枝子也被折断。
chapters.en: The LORD called thy name, A green olive tree, fair, of goodly fruit: with the noise of a great tumult he hath kindled fire upon it, and the branches of it are broken.

chapters.zh: 原来栽培你的万军之耶和华已经说，要降祸攻击你，是因以色列家和犹大家行恶，向巴力烧香，惹我发怒，是自作自受。
chapters.en: For the LORD of hosts, that planted thee, hath pronounced evil against thee, for the evil of the house of Israel and of the house of Judah, which they have done against themselves to provoke me to anger in offering incense unto Baal.

chapters.zh: 耶和华指示我，我就知道；你将他们所行的给我指明。
chapters.en: And the LORD hath given me knowledge , and I know : then thou shewedst me their doings.

chapters.zh: 我却像柔顺的羊羔被牵到宰杀之地；我并不知道他们设计谋害我，说：我们把树连果子都灭了罢！将他从活人之地剪除，使他的名不再被纪念。
chapters.en: But I like a lamb an ox is brought to the slaughter; and I knew not that they had devised devices against me, , Let us destroy the tree with the fruit thereof, and let us cut him off from the land of the living, that his name may be no more remembered.

chapters.zh: 按公义判断、察验人肺腑心肠的万军之耶和华啊，我却要见你在他们身上报仇，因我将我的案件向你禀明了。
chapters.en: But, O LORD of hosts, that judgest righteously, that triest the reins and the heart, let me see thy vengeance on them: for unto thee have I revealed my cause.

chapters.zh: 所以，耶和华论到寻索你命的亚拿突人如此说：他们说：你不要奉耶和华的名说预言，免得你死在我们手中。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD of the men of Anathoth, that seek thy life, saying, Prophesy not in the name of the LORD, that thou die not by our hand:

chapters.zh: 所以万军之耶和华如此说：看哪，我必刑罚他们；他们的少年人必被刀剑杀死，他们的儿女必因饥荒灭亡，
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD of hosts, Behold, I will punish them: the young men shall die by the sword; their sons and their daughters shall die by famine:

chapters.zh: 并且没有余剩的人留给他们；因为在追讨之年，我必使灾祸临到亚拿突人。
chapters.en: And there shall be no remnant of them: for I will bring evil upon the men of Anathoth, the year of their visitation.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我与你争辩的时候，你显为义；但有一件，我还要与你理论：恶人的道路为何亨通呢？大行诡诈的为何得安逸呢？
chapters.en: Righteous thou, O LORD, when I plead with thee: yet let me talk with thee of judgments: Wherefore doth the way of the wicked prosper? are all they happy that deal very treacherously?

chapters.zh: 你栽培了他们，他们也扎了根，长大，而且结果。他们的口是与你相近，心却与你远离。
chapters.en: Thou hast planted them, yea, they have taken root: they grow, yea, they bring forth fruit: thou near in their mouth, and far from their reins.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，你晓得我，看见我，察验我向你是怎样的心。求你将他们拉出来，好像将宰的羊，叫他们等候杀戮的日子。
chapters.en: But thou, O LORD, knowest me: thou hast seen me, and tried mine heart toward thee: pull them out like sheep for the slaughter, and prepare them for the day of slaughter.

chapters.zh: 这地悲哀，通国的青草枯干，要到几时呢？因其上居民的恶行，牲畜和飞鸟都灭绝了。他们曾说：他看不见我们的结局。
chapters.en: How long shall the land mourn, and the herbs of every field wither, for the wickedness of them that dwell therein? the beasts are consumed, and the birds; because they said, He shall not see our last end.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：你若与步行的人同跑，尚且觉累，怎能与马赛跑呢？你在平安之地，虽然安稳，在约但河边的丛林要怎样行呢？
chapters.en: If thou hast run with the footmen, and they have wearied thee, then how canst thou contend with horses? and in the land of peace, thou trustedst, , then how wilt thou do in the swelling of Jordan?

chapters.zh: 因为连你弟兄和你父家都用奸诈待你。他们也在你后边大声喊叫，虽向你说好话，你也不要信他们。
chapters.en: For even thy brethren, and the house of thy father, even they have dealt treacherously with thee; yea, they have called a multitude after thee: believe them not, though they speak fair words unto thee.

chapters.zh: 我离了我的殿宇，撇弃我的产业，将我心里所亲爱的交在他仇敌的手中。
chapters.en: I have forsaken mine house, I have left mine heritage; I have given the dearly beloved of my soul into the hand of her enemies.

chapters.zh: 我的产业向我如林中的狮子，他发声攻击我，因此我恨恶他。
chapters.en: Mine heritage is unto me as a lion in the forest; it crieth out against me: therefore have I hated it.

chapters.zh: 我的产业向我岂如斑点的鸷鸟呢？鸷鸟岂在他四围攻击他呢？你们要去聚集田野的百兽，带来吞吃罢！
chapters.en: Mine heritage unto me a speckled bird, the birds round about against her; come ye, assemble all the beasts of the field, come to devour.

chapters.zh: 许多牧人毁坏我的葡萄园，践踏我的分，使我美好的分变为荒凉的旷野。
chapters.en: Many pastors have destroyed my vineyard, they have trodden my portion under foot, they have made my pleasant portion a desolate wilderness.

chapters.zh: 他们使地荒凉；地既荒凉，便向我悲哀。全地荒凉，因无人介意。
chapters.en: They have made it desolate, desolate it mourneth unto me; the whole land is made desolate, because no man layeth to heart.

chapters.zh: 灭命的都来到旷野中一切净光的高处；耶和华的刀从地这边直到地那边尽行杀灭。凡有血气的都不得平安。
chapters.en: The spoilers are come upon all high places through the wilderness: for the sword of the LORD shall devour from the end of the land even to the end of the land: no flesh shall have peace.

chapters.zh: 他们种的是麦子，收的是荆棘；劳劳苦苦却毫无益处。因耶和华的烈怒，你们必为自己的土产羞愧。
chapters.en: They have sown wheat, but shall reap thorns: they have put themselves to pain, shall not profit: and they shall be ashamed of your revenues because of the fierce anger of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：一切恶邻，就是占据我使百姓以色列所承巡菇业的，我要将他们拔出本地，又要将犹大家从他们中间拔出来。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD against all mine evil neighbours, that touch the inheritance which I have caused my people Israel to inherit; Behold, I will pluck them out of their land, and pluck out the house of Judah from among them.

chapters.zh: 我拔出他们以后，我必转过来怜悯他们，把他们再带回来，各归本业，各归故土。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, after that I have plucked them out I will return, and have compassion on them, and will bring them again, every man to his heritage, and every man to his land.

chapters.zh: 他们若殷勤学习我百姓的道，指著我的名起誓说：我指著永生的耶和华起誓，正如他们从前教我百姓指著巴力起誓，他们就必建立在我百姓中间。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, if they will diligently learn the ways of my people, to swear by my name, The LORD liveth; as they taught my people to swear by Baal; then shall they be built in the midst of my people.

chapters.zh: 他们若是不听，我必拔出那国，拔出而且毁灭。这是耶和华说的。
chapters.en: But if they will not obey, I will utterly pluck up and destroy that nation, saith the LORD.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我如此说：你去买一根麻布带子束腰，不可放在水中。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD unto me, Go and get thee a linen girdle, and put it upon thy loins, and put it not in water.

chapters.zh: 我就照著耶和华的话，买了一根带子束腰。
chapters.en: So I got a girdle according to the word of the LORD, and put on my loins.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话第二次临到我说：
chapters.en: And the word of the LORD came unto me the second time, saying,

chapters.zh: 要拿著你所买的腰带，就是你腰上的带子，起来往伯拉河去，将腰带藏在那里的磐石穴中。
chapters.en: Take the girdle that thou hast got, which upon thy loins, and arise, go to Euphrates, and hide it there in a hole of the rock.

chapters.zh: 我就去，照著耶和华所吩咐我的，将腰带藏在伯拉河边。
chapters.en: So I went, and hid it by Euphrates, as the LORD commanded me.

chapters.zh: 过了多日，耶和华对我说：你起来往伯拉河去，将我吩咐你藏在那里的腰带取出来。
chapters.en: And it came to pass after many days, that the LORD said unto me, Arise, go to Euphrates, and take the girdle from thence, which I commanded thee to hide there.

chapters.zh: 我就往伯拉河去，将腰带从我所藏的地方刨出来，见腰带已经变坏，毫无用了。
chapters.en: Then I went to Euphrates, and digged, and took the girdle from the place where I had hid it: and, behold, the girdle was marred, it was profitable for nothing.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的话临到我说：
chapters.en: Then the word of the LORD came unto me, saying,

chapters.zh: 耶和华如此说：我必照样败坏犹大的骄傲和耶路撒冷的大骄傲。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD, After this manner will I mar the pride of Judah, and the great pride of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 这恶民不肯听我的话，按自己顽梗的心而行，随从别神，事奉敬拜，他们也必像这腰带变为无用。
chapters.en: This evil people, which refuse to hear my words, which walk in the imagination of their heart, and walk after other gods, to serve them, and to worship them, shall even be as this girdle, which is good for nothing.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：腰带怎样紧贴人腰，照样，我也使以色列全家和犹大全家紧贴我，好叫他们属我为子民，使我得名声，得颂赞，得荣耀；他们却不肯听。
chapters.en: For as the girdle cleaveth to the loins of a man, so have I caused to cleave unto me the whole house of Israel and the whole house of Judah, saith the LORD; that they might be unto me for a people, and for a name, and for a praise, and for a glory: but they would not hear.

chapters.zh: 所以你要对他们说：耶和华 ─ 以色列的神如此说：各坛都要盛满了酒。他们必对你说：我们岂不确知各坛都要盛满了酒呢？
chapters.en: Therefore thou shalt speak unto them this word; Thus saith the LORD God of Israel, Every bottle shall be filled with wine: and they shall say unto thee, Do we not certainly know that every bottle shall be filled with wine?

chapters.zh: 你就要对他们说：耶和华如此说：我必使这地的一切居民，就是坐大卫宝座的君王和祭司，与先知，并耶路撒冷的一切居民，都酩酊大醉。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou say unto them, Thus saith the LORD, Behold, I will fill all the inhabitants of this land, even the kings that sit upon David's throne, and the priests, and the prophets, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, with drunkenness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我要使他们彼此相碰，就是父与子彼此相碰；我必不可怜，不顾惜，不怜悯，以致灭绝他们。
chapters.en: And I will dash them one against another, even the fathers and the sons together, saith the LORD: I will not pity, nor spare, nor have mercy, but destroy them.

chapters.zh: 你们当听，当侧耳而听。不要骄傲，因为耶和华已经说了。
chapters.en: Hear ye, and give ear; be not proud: for the LORD hath spoken.

chapters.zh: 耶和华 ─ 你们的神未使黑暗来到，你们的脚未在昏暗山上绊跌之先，当将荣耀归给他；免得你们盼望光明，他使光明变为死荫，成为幽暗。
chapters.en: Give glory to the LORD your God, before he cause darkness, and before your feet stumble upon the dark mountains, and, while ye look for light, he turn it into the shadow of death, make gross darkness.

chapters.zh: 你们若不听这话，我必因你们的骄傲在暗地哭泣；我眼必痛哭流泪，因为耶和华的群众被掳去了。
chapters.en: But if ye will not hear it, my soul shall weep in secret places for pride; and mine eye shall weep sore, and run down with tears, because the LORD'S flock is carried away captive.

chapters.zh: 你要对君王和太后说：你们当自卑，坐在下边；因你们的头巾，就是你们的华冠，已经脱落了。
chapters.en: Say unto the king and to the queen, Humble yourselves, sit down: for your principalities shall come down, the crown of your glory.

chapters.zh: 南方的城尽都关闭，无人开放；犹大全被掳掠，且掳掠净尽。
chapters.en: The cities of the south shall be shut up, and none shall open : Judah shall be carried away captive all of it, it shall be wholly carried away captive.

chapters.zh: 你们要举目观看从北方来的人。先前赐给你的群众，就是你佳美的群众，如今在哪里呢？
chapters.en: Lift up your eyes, and behold them that come from the north: where the flock was given thee, thy beautiful flock?

chapters.zh: 耶和华立你自己所交的朋友为首，辖制你，那时你还有甚么话说呢？痛苦岂不将你抓住像产难的妇人么？
chapters.en: What wilt thou say when he shall punish thee? for thou hast taught them captains, as chief over thee: shall not sorrows take thee, as a woman in travail?

chapters.zh: 你若心里说：这一切事为何临到我呢？你的衣襟揭起，你的脚跟受伤，是因你的罪孽甚多。
chapters.en: And if thou say in thine heart, Wherefore come these things upon me? For the greatness of thine iniquity are thy skirts discovered, thy heels made bare.

chapters.zh: 古实人岂能改变皮肤呢？豹岂能改变斑点呢？若能，你们这习惯行恶的便能行善了。
chapters.en: Can the Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots? may ye also do good, that are accustomed to do evil.

chapters.zh: 所以我必用旷野的风吹散他们，像吹过的碎秸一样。
chapters.en: Therefore will I scatter them as the stubble that passeth away by the wind of the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：这是你所当得的，是我量给你的分；因为你忘记我，倚靠虚假（或译：偶像）。
chapters.en: This thy lot, the portion of thy measures from me, saith the LORD; because thou hast forgotten me, and trusted in falsehood.

chapters.zh: 所以我要揭起你的衣襟，蒙在你脸上，显出你的丑陋。
chapters.en: Therefore will I discover thy skirts upon thy face, that thy shame may appear.

chapters.zh: 你那些可憎恶之事 ─ 就是在田野的山上行奸淫，发嘶声，做淫乱的事 ─ 我都看见了。耶路撒冷啊，你有祸了！你不肯洁净，还要到几时呢？
chapters.en: I have seen thine adulteries, and thy neighings, the lewdness of thy whoredom, thine abominations on the hills in the fields. Woe unto thee, O Jerusalem! wilt thou not be made clean? when once ?

chapters.zh: 耶和华论到干旱之灾的话临到耶利米：
chapters.en: The word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah concerning the dearth.

chapters.zh: 犹大悲哀，城门衰败。众人披上黑衣坐在地上；耶路撒冷的哀声上达。
chapters.en: Judah mourneth, and the gates thereof languish; they are black unto the ground; and the cry of Jerusalem is gone up.

chapters.zh: 他们的贵胄打发家僮打水；他们来到水池，见没有水，就拿著空器皿，蒙羞惭愧，抱头而回。
chapters.en: And their nobles have sent their little ones to the waters: they came to the pits, found no water; they returned with their vessels empty; they were ashamed and confounded, and covered their heads.

chapters.zh: 耕地的也蒙羞抱头；因为无雨降在地上，地都干裂。
chapters.en: Because the ground is chapt, for there was no rain in the earth, the plowmen were ashamed, they covered their heads.

chapters.zh: 田野的母鹿生下小鹿，就撇弃，因为无草。
chapters.en: Yea, the hind also calved in the field, and forsook , because there was no grass.

chapters.zh: 野驴站在净光的高处，喘气好像野狗；因为无草，眼目失明。
chapters.en: And the wild asses did stand in the high places, they snuffed up the wind like dragons; their eyes did fail, because no grass.

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我们的罪孽虽然作见证告我们，还求你为你名的缘故行事。我们本是多次背道，得罪了你。
chapters.en: O LORD, though our iniquities testify against us, do thou for thy name's sake: for our backslidings are many; we have sinned against thee.

chapters.zh: 以色列所盼望、在患难时作他救主的啊，你为何在这地像寄居的，又像行路的只住一宵呢？
chapters.en: O the hope of Israel, the saviour thereof in time of trouble, why shouldest thou be as a stranger in the land, and as a wayfaring man turneth aside to tarry for a night?

chapters.zh: 你为何像受惊的人，像不能救人的勇士呢？耶和华啊，你仍在我们中间；我们也称为你名下的人，求你不要离开我们。
chapters.en: Why shouldest thou be as a man astonied, as a mighty man cannot save? yet thou, O LORD, in the midst of us, and we are called by thy name; leave us not.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对这百姓如此说：这百姓喜爱妄行（原文是飘流），不禁止脚步，所以耶和华不悦纳他们。现今要记念他们的罪孽，追讨他们的罪恶。
chapters.en: Thus saith the LORD unto this people, Thus have they loved to wander, they have not refrained their feet, therefore the LORD doth not accept them; he will now remember their iniquity, and visit their sins.

chapters.zh: 耶和华又对我说：不要为这百姓祈祷求好处。
chapters.en: Then said the LORD unto me, Pray not for this people for good.

chapters.zh: 他们禁食的时候，我不听他们的呼求；他们献燔祭和素祭，我也不悦纳；我却要用刀剑、饥荒、瘟疫灭绝他们。
chapters.en: When they fast, I will not hear their cry; and when they offer burnt offering and an oblation, I will not accept them: but I will consume them by the sword, and by the famine, and by the pestilence.

chapters.zh: 我就说：唉！主耶和华啊，那些先知常对他们说：你们必不看见刀剑，也不遭遇饥荒；耶和华要在这地方赐你们长久的平安。
chapters.en: Then said I, Ah, Lord GOD! behold, the prophets say unto them, Ye shall not see the sword, neither shall ye have famine; but I will give you assured peace in this place.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对我说：那些先知托我的名说假预言，我并没有打发他们，没有吩咐他们，也没有对他们说话；他们向你们预言的，乃是虚假的异象和占卜，并虚无的事，以及本心的诡诈。
chapters.en: Then the LORD said unto me, The prophets prophesy lies in my name: I sent them not, neither have I commanded them, neither spake unto them: they prophesy unto you a false vision and divination, and a thing of nought, and the deceit of their heart.

chapters.zh: 所以耶和华如此说：论到托我名说预言的那些先知，我并没有打发他们；他们还说这地不能有刀剑饥荒，其实那些先知必被刀剑饥荒灭绝。
chapters.en: Therefore thus saith the LORD concerning the prophets that prophesy in my name, and I sent them not, yet they say, Sword and famine shall not be in this land; By sword and famine shall those prophets be consumed.

chapters.zh: 听他们说预言的百姓必因饥荒刀剑抛在耶路撒冷的街道上，无人葬埋。他们连妻子带儿女，都是如此。我必将他们的恶倒在他们身上（或译：我必使他们罪恶的报应临到他们身上）。
chapters.en: And the people to whom they prophesy shall be cast out in the streets of Jerusalem because of the famine and the sword; and they shall have none to bury them, them, their wives, nor their sons, nor their daughters: for I will pour their wickedness upon them.

chapters.zh: 你要将这话对他们说：愿我眼泪汪汪，昼夜不息，因为我百姓（原文是民的处女）受了裂口破坏的大伤。
chapters.en: Therefore thou shalt say this word unto them; Let mine eyes run down with tears night and day, and let them not cease: for the virgin daughter of my people is broken with a great breach, with a very grievous blow.

chapters.zh: 我若出往田间，就见有被刀杀的；我若进入城内，就见有因饥荒患病的；连先知带祭司在国中往来，也是毫无知识（或译：不知怎样才好）。
chapters.en: If I go forth into the field, then behold the slain with the sword! and if I enter into the city, then behold them that are sick with famine! yea, both the prophet and the priest go about into a land that they know not.

chapters.zh: 你全然弃掉犹大么？你心厌恶锡安么？为何击打我们，以致无法医治呢？我们指望平安，却得不著好处；指望痊愈，不料，受了惊惶。
chapters.en: Hast thou utterly rejected Judah? hath thy soul lothed Zion? why hast thou smitten us, and no healing for us? we looked for peace, and no good; and for the time of healing, and behold trouble!

chapters.zh: 耶和华啊，我们承认自己的罪恶，和我们列祖的罪孽，因我们得罪了你。
